intent: Uncomment and fix the delta.tool_calls handler in nvidia.rs to emit StreamToken::ToolCall for both delta.tool_calls and delta.function_call formats. Also update integration test debug print format.
residual_risks: Silent JSON parse errors in tool call arguments may mask malformed data from LLM provider
UUID generation inconsistency between tool_calls and function_call paths may affect tool call tracking

requirements.description: stream_complete must emit StreamToken::ToolCall for both delta.tool_calls and delta.function_call formats
requirements.evidence: Lines 380-396 handle delta.tool_calls, lines 399-414 handle delta.function_call, both emit StreamToken::ToolCall

requirements.description: Integration test must load real LLM credentials from ~/.bos/conf/config.toml via ConfigLoader
Integration test must send a request that triggers tool calls and verify the tokens are correctly emitted
requirements.evidence: Test collects StreamToken::ToolCall and verifies has_tool_call is true

learnings: Plan document at docs/plans/2026-04-25-001-feat-nvidia-tool-calls-streaming-plan.md provides comprehensive context
OpenAI vendor implementation serves as reference pattern for delta.tool_calls handling
Integration tests follow established pattern of gating on real credentials presence

coverage.notes: Sub-agents timed out after 30 minutes. Manual review completed instead.

verdict.reasoning: All requirements from the plan are met. The implementation correctly uncomments and fixes the delta.tool_calls handler, matching the pattern from the function_call handler. Integration test properly validates the feature. Minor code quality notes (UUID inconsistency, duplication) do not block merge.
